steps.title: Set up the conditional probability
steps.content:
We want $P(\text{age} > 65 \mid \text{age} \geq 18)$. By definition:

$$P(>65 \mid \geq 18) = \frac{\#\text{ people }>65}{\#\text{ people }\geq 18}$$

So we restrict the denominator to adults (18 and older), then take the count over 65 within that group.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content:
Plugging in the table values, the over-65 count divided by the at-least-18 count gives a ratio closest to 0.29.

0.21 understates and matches the unconditional fraction; 0.33 and 0.73 are too high (0.73 is roughly the proportion of adults among everyone, not the conditional probability we want).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
